title: Caucalis maxima
taxonomy: Caucalis maxima is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly known as the 'big horned caucalis'. This plant is classified within the genus Caucalis, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Caucalis maxima is further classified within the tribe Caucalideae, a group of plants closely related to the carrot family. The plant's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus.
uses: Caucalis maxima is also known as Large Horned Poppy or Maxim's Cuckooflower, but information on its medicinal uses is limited. However, it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant's root and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, and it is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues and fever. Additionally, Caucalis maxima is said to have antimicrobial properties, which may help to combat infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.

intro: Caucalis maxima, also known as Maxim's mouse ear or large mouse-ear, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective effects. Bioactive constituents of Caucalis maxima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, improve liver function, and reduce inflammation.
